summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates host identifiers and environment variables to webhook.site
details: On npm install, the package's postinstall hook executes index.js, which collects the installer's hostname, whoami output, /etc/hostname, directory listings of /opt, /root, and $HOME, and the full process.env, then POSTs the aggregated JSON payload over HTTPS to a hardcoded webhook.site endpoint (https://webhook.site/5212190f-c317-4185-ab7b-b4f106ed56d3). Bulk exfiltration of process.env from CI/build environments routinely captures credentials such as AWS keys, npm tokens, and other build secrets. There is no legitimate purpose consistent with the package metadata for this behavior; it is a classic install-time reconnaissance and credential-harvest payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
